name: LDtk
description: Modern 2D level editor by the Dead Cells developer. Open-source, cross-platform, with auto-layers, entity placement, world view, and JSON export compatible with all major 2D engines.

seo.title: LDtk — Game Development Tools
seo.meta_description: LDtk: Modern 2D level editor by the Dead Cells developer. Open-source, cross-platform, with auto-layers, entity placement, world view, and JSON export compatibl

when_to_use: 2D RPG world map built with auto-layers in LDtk editor
Platformer level designed and exported as JSON to Godot
Dead Cells-style tilemap integration in Unity 2D project
Entity placement in level tied to game data via LDtk schema
Open-source level editor used to avoid proprietary lock-in

pros: Modern UX runs circles around Tiled
Auto-layers rules cut manual tile placement massively
JSON export is clean and schema is stable
Free and open source with active solo maintainer
cons: Single maintainer means truck factor of one
Ecosystem smaller than Tileds battle-tested community
Importers for some engines lag behind Tiled coverage
No isometric or hex tile support out of the box
review.verdict: The modern choice for 2D level editing; switch from Tiled unless you specifically need hex or isometric.
review.best_for: Indies making orthogonal 2D games wanting a modern level editor with auto-layers
review.not_for: Hex grids, isometric projects, or teams demanding a huge contributor base

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Free / hobby tier; no peak-lock evidence.
